% This document was generated by an LLM.

\documentclass{essay}

\title{Exercises on Inequalities for Calculus}
\author{}
\date{}

\begin{document}

\maketitle

\section*{Level 1 --- Algebraic Inequalities}

\begin{enumerate}
    \item Prove that for every real number \(x\),
    \[
        x^2 + 1 \ge 2x.
    \]

    \item Show that
    \[
        (a-b)^2 \ge 0
    \]
    implies
    \[
        \frac{a+b}{2} \ge \sqrt{ab},
        \qquad a,b>0.
    \]

    \item Prove that
    \[
        |a+b| \le |a|+|b|.
    \]

    \item Prove that
    \[
        \bigl||a|-|b|\bigr| \le |a-b|.
    \]

    \item If \(0<x<1\), prove that
    \[
        x^2 < x < \sqrt{x}.
    \]

    \item If \(x>0\), prove that
    \[
        x+\frac{1}{x}\ge2.
    \]
\end{enumerate}

\section*{Level 2 --- Inequalities Involving Elementary Functions}

\begin{enumerate}[resume]

    \item Show that for every real number \(x\),
    \[
        |\sin x| \le |x|.
    \]

    \item Show that
    \[
        1-\cos x \le \frac{x^2}{2}.
    \]

    \item For every real number \(x\), prove that
    \[
        e^x \ge 1+x.
    \]

    \item Prove that for \(x>-1\),
    \[
        \ln(1+x)\le x.
    \]

    \item Show that for \(x>0\),
    \[
        \ln x \le x-1.
    \]

    \item Prove that for every real number \(x\),
    \[
        \cosh x \ge 1+\frac{x^2}{2}.
    \]
\end{enumerate}

\section*{Level 3 --- Bounding Sequences}

\begin{enumerate}[resume]

    \item Prove that
    \[
        \frac{1}{n+1}
        <
        \ln\!\left(1+\frac{1}{n}\right)
        <
        \frac{1}{n}.
    \]

    \item Show that
    \[
        \left(1+\frac{1}{n}\right)^n < e.
    \]

    \item Prove that
    \[
        \left(1+\frac{1}{n}\right)^n
        <
        \left(1+\frac{1}{n+1}\right)^{n+1}.
    \]

    \item Show that
    \[
        \sum_{k=1}^{n}\frac{1}{k}
        >
        \ln(n+1).
    \]

    \item Prove that
    \[
        \sum_{k=1}^{n}\frac{1}{k}
        <
        1+\ln n.
    \]
\end{enumerate}

\section*{Level 4 --- Integral Inequalities}

\begin{enumerate}[resume]

    \item If \(f(x)\ge0\) on \([a,b]\), prove that
    \[
        \int_a^b f(x)\,dx \ge 0.
    \]

    \item If
    \[
        f(x)\le g(x)
    \]
    on \([a,b]\), prove that
    \[
        \int_a^b f(x)\,dx
        \le
        \int_a^b g(x)\,dx.
    \]

    \item Prove that
    \[
        \sin x
        <
        x
        <
        \tan x,
        \qquad
        0<x<\frac{\pi}{2}.
    \]

    \item Show that
    \[
        \frac{x}{1+x}
        \le
        \ln(1+x)
        \le
        x,
        \qquad
        x>0.
    \]

    \item Let \(f:[a,b]\to\mathbb{R}\) satisfy \(f(x)>0\) for all \(x\in[a,b]\). Prove that
    \[
        \frac{b-a}{\max f}
        \le
        \int_a^b \frac{1}{f(x)}\,dx.
    \]

    \item Let \(f\) be integrable on \([a,b]\). Prove the triangle inequality for integrals:
    \[
        \left|\int_a^b f(x)\,dx\right|
        \le
        \int_a^b |f(x)|\,dx.
    \]

    \item Let \(f,g\) be integrable on \([a,b]\). Prove the Cauchy--Schwarz inequality for integrals:
    \[
        \left(\int_a^b f(x)g(x)\,dx\right)^{\!2}
        \le
        \left(\int_a^b f(x)^2\,dx\right)
        \left(\int_a^b g(x)^2\,dx\right).
    \]
\end{enumerate}

\section*{Level 5 --- Inequalities Used in Convergence Proofs}

\begin{enumerate}[resume]

    \item Determine for which values of \(p\),
    \[
        \frac{1}{n^p}\le\frac{1}{n}.
    \]

    \item Show that
    \[
        \frac{n}{n^2+1}<\frac{1}{n}.
    \]

    \item Find a constant \(C\) such that, near the origin,
    \[
        |\sin x - x|
        \le
        C|x|^3.
    \]

    \item Prove that
    \[
        \left|\frac{\sin x}{x}\right|
        \le
        1,
        \qquad
        x\neq0.
    \]

    \item Show that
    \[
        \frac{1}{n+1}
        <
        \ln(n+1)-\ln n
        <
        \frac{1}{n}.
    \]

    \item Use inequalities to prove that
    \[
        \lim_{n\to\infty}\frac{\ln n}{n}=0.
    \]
\end{enumerate}

\section*{Level 6 --- Classical Inequalities}

\begin{enumerate}[resume]

    \item Prove the Arithmetic Mean--Geometric Mean (AM--GM) inequality: for nonnegative real numbers \(a_1,\dots,a_n\),
    \[
        \frac{a_1+a_2+\cdots+a_n}{n}
        \ge
        \sqrt[n]{a_1 a_2 \cdots a_n}.
    \]

    \item Prove the Cauchy--Schwarz inequality: for real numbers \(a_1,\dots,a_n\) and \(b_1,\dots,b_n\),
    \[
        \left(\sum_{i=1}^n a_i b_i\right)^{\!2}
        \le
        \left(\sum_{i=1}^n a_i^2\right)
        \left(\sum_{i=1}^n b_i^2\right).
    \]

    \item Prove Hölder's inequality: for \(p,q>1\) with \(\dfrac{1}{p}+\dfrac{1}{q}=1\),
    \[
        \sum_{i=1}^n |a_i b_i|
        \le
        \left(\sum_{i=1}^n |a_i|^p\right)^{\!1/p}
        \left(\sum_{i=1}^n |b_i|^q\right)^{\!1/q}.
    \]

    \item Prove Minkowski's inequality: for \(p\ge1\),
    \[
        \left(\sum_{i=1}^n |a_i+b_i|^p\right)^{\!1/p}
        \le
        \left(\sum_{i=1}^n |a_i|^p\right)^{\!1/p}
        +
        \left(\sum_{i=1}^n |b_i|^p\right)^{\!1/p}.
    \]

    \item Prove Jensen's inequality: if \(f\) is convex, then for \(x_1,\dots,x_n\),
    \[
        f\!\left(\frac{1}{n}\sum_{i=1}^n x_i\right)
        \le
        \frac{1}{n}\sum_{i=1}^n f(x_i).
    \]

    \item Prove Bernoulli's inequality:
    \[
        (1+x)^r \ge 1+rx,
    \]
    for the appropriate values of \(r\) and \(x\).

    \item Prove Young's inequality:
    \[
        ab
        \le
        \frac{a^p}{p}
        +
        \frac{b^q}{q},
        \qquad
        \frac{1}{p}+\frac{1}{q}=1.
    \]

    \item Prove the rearrangement inequality: if \(a_1\le a_2\le\cdots\le a_n\) and \(b_1\le b_2\le\cdots\le b_n\), then for any permutation \(\sigma\) of \(\{1,\dots,n\}\),
    \[
        \sum_{i=1}^n a_i b_{n+1-i}
        \le
        \sum_{i=1}^n a_i b_{\sigma(i)}
        \le
        \sum_{i=1}^n a_i b_i.
    \]

    \item Prove Chebyshev's sum inequality: if \(a_1\le a_2\le\cdots\le a_n\) and \(b_1\le b_2\le\cdots\le b_n\), then
    \[
        \frac{1}{n}\sum_{i=1}^n a_i b_i
        \ge
        \left(\frac{1}{n}\sum_{i=1}^n a_i\right)
        \left(\frac{1}{n}\sum_{i=1}^n b_i\right).
    \]

    \item Prove the power mean inequality: for positive reals \(a_1,\dots,a_n\) and nonzero exponents \(r<s\), the power means
    \[
        M_r=\left(\frac{1}{n}\sum_{i=1}^n a_i^r\right)^{\!1/r}
    \]
    satisfy
    \[
        M_r \le M_s.
    \]

    \item Prove Nesbitt's inequality: for positive reals \(a,b,c\),
    \[
        \frac{a}{b+c}+\frac{b}{a+c}+\frac{c}{a+b}
        \ge
        \frac{3}{2}.
    \]
\end{enumerate}

\section*{Level 7 --- Inequalities from Taylor's Theorem}

\begin{enumerate}[resume]

    \item Let \(f\) be \((n+1)\)-times differentiable on an interval containing \(a\) and \(x\), with \(|f^{(n+1)}(t)|\le M\) for all \(t\) between \(a\) and \(x\). Prove Taylor's inequality:
    \[
        \left|
        f(x) - \sum_{k=0}^{n}\frac{f^{(k)}(a)}{k!}(x-a)^k
        \right|
        \le
        \frac{M}{(n+1)!}|x-a|^{n+1}.
    \]

    \item Use Taylor's inequality to show that for \(0\le x\le1\),
    \[
        \left|
        e^x - \left(1+x+\frac{x^2}{2}\right)
        \right|
        \le
        \frac{e}{6}x^3.
    \]

    \item Use Taylor's inequality to show that for every real number \(x\),
    \[
        \left|
        \sin x - \left(x-\frac{x^3}{6}\right)
        \right|
        \le
        \frac{x^4}{24}.
    \]
\end{enumerate}

\end{document}
